title: Cough: dry versus productive
summary: A cough is a protective reflex, not a diagnosis. A dry, irritating cough may respond to soothing or short-term suppression, while a productive cough is usually better managed by hydration, movement and treating the cause rather than trying to stop all coughing.
evidenceSnapshot: Most acute viral coughs improve without specific treatment, although a post-viral cough can last 2–3 weeks. Evidence for OTC cough medicines is inconsistent.

interventions.name: Honey for people older than 12 months
interventions.finding: May modestly reduce night-time cough and improve sleep, especially in children, but it does not cure the infection.
interventions.practical: Use a small spoonful directly or in a warm drink before bed. Exact amounts have varied across trials.
interventions.mechanism: Coats and soothes irritated throat tissue; may have mild antimicrobial activity.
interventions.cautions: Never give honey to a child under 12 months. Consider sugar exposure and diabetes.

interventions.name: Warm fluids and throat soothing
interventions.finding: Warm drinks, lozenges and frequent sips can reduce throat-triggered coughing and improve comfort.
interventions.practical: Sip regularly; avoid drinks hot enough to burn.
interventions.mechanism: Moistens irritated mucosa and may reduce cough-receptor stimulation.
interventions.cautions: Use age-appropriate lozenges; choking risk in young children.

interventions.name: Dextromethorphan
interventions.finding: Some adults report short-term reduction in dry cough, but systematic-review evidence is inconsistent and benefit is often small.
interventions.practical: Use only for a troublesome dry cough and follow the product label; check all combination ingredients.
interventions.mechanism: Acts centrally to reduce the cough reflex.
interventions.cautions: Avoid combining with interacting serotonergic medicines without pharmacist advice; may cause drowsiness, dizziness or misuse.

interventions.name: Guaifenesin
interventions.finding: May make mucus easier to cough up for some adults, but evidence for acute viral cough is limited.
interventions.practical: Take with adequate fluid and follow the label.
interventions.mechanism: Intended to increase hydration of airway secretions.
interventions.cautions: Not a substitute for assessment when breathless, wheezing or coughing blood.

interventions.name: Routine cough mixtures in children
interventions.finding: They do not reliably shorten illness and can cause harm; Australian guidance advises against decongestants, antihistamines and cough syrups for children with ordinary colds.
interventions.practical: Use saline, fluids, comfort and age/weight-appropriate pain relief instead.
interventions.cautions: Do not improvise adult products or doses for children.

practicalSteps: Decide whether the cough is mainly dry/tickly or wet/productive.
For a wet cough, encourage fluids and gentle upright activity; do not aim to suppress every cough.
For a dry night cough, use warm fluid, honey if over 12 months, and elevate the upper body slightly.
Keep smoke, vaping, fragrances and cold dry air away from irritated airways.
avoidOrCare: Seek pharmacist or doctor advice before cough suppressants if pregnant, breastfeeding, taking antidepressants, or living with asthma/COPD.
Green or yellow phlegm alone does not prove a bacterial infection.
A cough after a cold can linger, but worsening breathlessness or new fever needs reassessment.

myths.claim: A productive cough should always be suppressed.
myths.reality: Coughing helps clear secretions. Suppression may be useful only when the cough is exhausting, painful or preventing sleep, and the person has no concerning symptoms.

myths.claim: Cough syrup makes a cold end faster.
myths.reality: Most products only target symptoms, and the overall evidence for acute cough mixtures is weak or inconsistent.

redFlags: Trouble breathing, blue lips, stridor, severe wheeze or chest pain
A child with chest recession, very fast breathing, poor feeding or fewer wet nappies
Cough lasting more than a few weeks, or improving then clearly worsening
unsettled: Which specific formulations and doses of dextromethorphan or guaifenesin provide a clinically meaningful benefit remains uncertain.
